ranges.label: Low Burnout
ranges.description: Your overall burnout score is low, suggesting you are managing your work and personal demands effectively. You do not appear to be experiencing significant physical or emotional exhaustion related to your daily activities.
suggestions: Continue maintaining a healthy work-life balance
Keep up strategies that help you manage stress and recharge
Be aware of early signs of burnout to maintain your current well-being

ranges.label: Moderate Burnout
ranges.description: Your overall burnout score suggests moderate levels of burnout. You may be experiencing some physical or emotional exhaustion that is affecting your energy, motivation, or satisfaction. Review your subscale scores to identify whether the primary source is personal, work-related, or client-related.
suggestions: Review your subscale scores to identify the primary source of burnout
Evaluate your current workload and set boundaries where possible
Prioritize self-care activities including rest, exercise, and social connection
Consider discussing workload concerns with your supervisor or mentor

ranges.label: Severe Burnout
ranges.description: Your overall burnout score is high, indicating severe burnout that is likely significantly affecting your physical health, emotional well-being, and professional functioning. At this level, meaningful changes to your work situation and/or professional support are important.
suggestions: Seek support from a mental health professional or employee assistance program
Evaluate whether structural changes to your work situation are possible and necessary
Prioritize recovery activities and set firm boundaries around work time
Consider discussing your situation with trusted colleagues or supervisors
Burnout is a response to chronic workplace stress, not a personal failure

personal.label: Low
personal.description: Low personal burnout. You do not appear to be experiencing significant general fatigue or exhaustion unrelated to work. Your overall energy and vitality appear adequate.

personal.label: Moderate
personal.description: Moderate personal burnout. You may be feeling somewhat physically and emotionally drained in general, regardless of work. This may reflect lifestyle factors, health issues, or the cumulative effect of multiple demands.

personal.label: High
personal.description: High personal burnout. You are likely experiencing significant physical and emotional exhaustion that pervades your daily life beyond just work. Attending to your overall health and self-care is important.

work.label: Low
work.description: Low work-related burnout. Your work does not appear to be causing you significant exhaustion or distress. You are likely managing your professional responsibilities effectively.

work.label: Moderate
work.description: Moderate work-related burnout. You are experiencing some exhaustion and strain directly related to your work. Workload, time pressure, or lack of resources may be contributing factors.

work.label: High
work.description: High work-related burnout. Your work is causing you significant physical and emotional exhaustion. Work conditions, demands, or lack of support may need to change. Consider discussing this with your supervisor or HR.

client.label: Low
client.description: Low client-related burnout. Working with clients, patients, or students does not appear to be causing you significant exhaustion. You are maintaining adequate emotional resources for interpersonal work.

client.label: Moderate
client.description: Moderate client-related burnout. You are experiencing some exhaustion specifically related to working with clients or patients. Compassion fatigue or emotional demands of interpersonal work may be contributing factors.

client.label: High
client.description: High client-related burnout. The emotional demands of working with clients or patients are causing you significant exhaustion. This may affect the quality of care you provide. Supervision, peer support, and self-care are essential.

ranges.label: Low Burnout
ranges.description: Your overall burnout score is low, suggesting healthy engagement with your work. You do not appear to be experiencing significant exhaustion or disengagement from your professional role.
suggestions: Continue maintaining a healthy relationship with your work
Preserve the strategies that help you stay engaged and energized
Be mindful of early signs of exhaustion or disengagement

ranges.label: Moderate Burnout
ranges.description: Your score suggests moderate levels of burnout. You may be experiencing some combination of exhaustion and disengagement from your work. Review your subscale scores to understand whether exhaustion, disengagement, or both are the primary concerns.
suggestions: Review subscale scores to identify whether exhaustion, disengagement, or both are most prominent
Evaluate your workload and consider adjustments to prevent further burnout
Engage in restorative activities outside of work to recharge
Talk to a trusted colleague, supervisor, or counselor about your experience

ranges.label: High Burnout
ranges.description: Your score indicates high burnout characterized by significant exhaustion and/or disengagement from your work. This level of burnout is likely affecting your professional performance, personal well-being, and relationships. Meaningful intervention is recommended.
suggestions: Seek professional support from a counselor, therapist, or employee assistance program
Evaluate whether systemic changes to your work situation are needed
Prioritize rest and recovery; burnout recovery requires sustained effort
Consider whether a temporary break or workload reduction is possible
Burnout is a systemic issue, not a personal weakness; structural solutions are often needed

exhaustion.label: Low
exhaustion.description: Low exhaustion. You do not appear to be experiencing significant physical, cognitive, or emotional fatigue related to your work. Your energy levels seem adequate for your professional demands.

exhaustion.label: Moderate
exhaustion.description: Moderate exhaustion. You are experiencing noticeable fatigue — physical, cognitive, or emotional — that may be affecting your capacity to perform at your best. Work demands may be exceeding your available resources.

exhaustion.label: High
exhaustion.description: High exhaustion. You are likely feeling severely drained by your work, with persistent fatigue that does not resolve with normal rest. This level of exhaustion can affect both your professional performance and personal health.

disengagement.label: Low
disengagement.description: Low disengagement. You appear to be actively engaged with and interested in your work. You find your professional role meaningful and are emotionally connected to your work activities.

disengagement.label: Moderate
disengagement.description: Moderate disengagement. You may be experiencing some emotional distancing from your work, reduced interest, or cynicism about your professional role. This can be an early warning sign of deepening burnout.

disengagement.label: High
disengagement.description: High disengagement. You are likely feeling emotionally detached from your work, with little interest or sense of meaning in your professional activities. This may manifest as cynicism, going through the motions, or withdrawing from work relationships.

ranges.label: Languishing
ranges.description: Your score suggests you are languishing — experiencing low levels of emotional, social, and psychological well-being. Languishing is characterized by a sense of emptiness, stagnation, and lack of engagement with life. While not the same as depression, languishing is associated with impaired functioning and lower quality of life.
suggestions: Consider consulting a mental health professional to explore your current experience
Engage in activities that foster connection, purpose, and personal growth
Small daily practices such as gratitude, social interaction, and physical activity can help shift toward greater well-being
Languishing is a recognized mental health state that can improve with intentional effort and support

ranges.label: Moderate Mental Health
ranges.description: Your score indicates moderate mental health — you are neither languishing nor flourishing. You experience a mix of positive and challenging aspects in your emotional, social, and psychological life. This is the most common category and represents an opportunity for growth.
suggestions: Identify areas of your life where you feel most and least fulfilled
Invest in activities and relationships that contribute to meaning and engagement
Review your subscale scores to see which dimensions of well-being could benefit from attention

ranges.label: Flourishing
ranges.description: Your score suggests you are flourishing — experiencing high levels of emotional well-being, positive social functioning, and psychological well-being. Flourishing is associated with greater resilience, productivity, and life satisfaction.
suggestions: Continue the practices, relationships, and activities that support your flourishing
Consider sharing your positive energy and strategies with others
Stay engaged in personal growth and meaningful pursuits

emotional.label: Low
emotional.description: Low emotional well-being. You may be experiencing infrequent positive emotions, low life satisfaction, or reduced interest in daily activities. This dimension reflects your capacity for happiness and contentment.

emotional.label: Moderate
emotional.description: Moderate emotional well-being. You experience some positive emotions and life satisfaction, but there may be room for growth in cultivating positive affect and contentment in your daily life.

emotional.label: High
emotional.description: High emotional well-being. You frequently experience positive emotions, happiness, and satisfaction with life. This emotional vitality is a valuable resource for resilience and overall health.

social.label: Low
social.description: Low social well-being. You may feel that you do not belong to or contribute meaningfully to your community. Social well-being reflects your sense of connection to and functioning within society.

social.label: Moderate
social.description: Moderate social well-being. You have some sense of social belonging and contribution, but may feel disconnected in certain areas of social life. Strengthening community ties and social engagement may help.

social.label: High
social.description: High social well-being. You feel a strong sense of belonging to your community, see society as meaningful, and feel you make a positive contribution. This social integration supports overall mental health.

psychological.label: Low
psychological.description: Low psychological well-being. You may be experiencing limited personal growth, reduced sense of purpose, or difficulty managing your environment. This dimension reflects autonomy, mastery, and self-acceptance.

psychological.label: Moderate
psychological.description: Moderate psychological well-being. You experience some sense of purpose, personal growth, and self-acceptance, but there may be areas where you feel less fulfilled or autonomous. Exploring your values and goals can help.

psychological.label: High
psychological.description: High psychological well-being. You have a strong sense of purpose, ongoing personal growth, positive self-regard, and autonomy. These psychological resources are protective factors for long-term mental health.
